name: Tests with tautological assertions and self-referential expectations

planted_issues.description: test_valid_record_parses_successfully calls parse_record but asserts `assert True` instead of checking the result. This test can never fail regardless of what parse_record returns or whether it raises an exception (as long as it doesn't raise). It provides false confidence.

test_batch_transformation_produces_correct_output computes expected output by calling the same function under test: `expected = transform_batch(input_batch)`. This means `assert result == expected` is equivalent to `assert transform_batch(x) == transform_batch(x)`, which always passes regardless of whether the function is correct.

There is no test for the error/exception path of parse_record or transform_batch with malformed data (e.g., wrong types, corrupt timestamps, None values). Only the happy path and explicit validation errors are tested. Silent data corruption would go undetected.

clean_areas.description: TestInputValidation properly tests boundary values (negative, zero, max, future timestamp) with specific exception matching and explicit assertions.
